[h1]It was by our hands that this series is once again given flesh[/h1]
Delivering the same level of addicting dopamine that made Vampire Survivors the smash hit that it was, this fun rogue-like combining dungeon crawling with deck building will make you want to be called here and wishing to pay tribute.

It's all in the cards - how you will fare in this game will mostly depend on what cards you decide to take with each level up. Each one boasts different effects when used, providing a surprising amount of depth. It was interesting to see how all the weapons and passives from VS were able to work as cards you use to fight enemies, along with some brand new ones. There's a bit of RNG involved and it can be frustrating when you're given bad hands, but fortunately this doesn't happen too often if you have a good deck.

The combo system allows for some insane plays once you understand how they work. Lower cost cards should be played first so they can then be followed up by the next cost up to increase the effects of the cards you use. It's not just what cards you play, but also the order you play them in - if you play your cards right, you can become insanely overpowered in no time. Cards that would otherwise see little value normally can become incredibly useful when played during high combos.
https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3715086879
If you're looking for some hidden gems, this game has you covered! Finding gems along the way will allow you to upgrade your cards with special effects when played such as doubling or tripling damage dealt, creating coin cards, or my personal favourite, turning them into wild cards that always chains combos and costs 0 mana to use! These are usually found in chests and provide just as much dopamine upon opening as paid loot boxes - the main difference being you're not spending any money to open these.

Coins earned during runs can be used to upgrade powerups, giving you the helping hand you might need if you're struggling on a particular area. Cards can also be upgraded at the blacksmith for a fee, adding extra gem slots for that particular card in future runs so it's worth it to try and make as much gold as possible which is just as satisfying as ever to do, especially once you unlock the overkill relics later on.

Despite how addicting this game can get, runs can sometimes take a long time. While VS had up to 30 minute runs, dungeons here can take at least an hour to complete so it doesn't quite have the pick up and play experience VS had. Fortunately, you don't have to do an entire run in one sitting as you can save your progress mid-run by quitting to the title screen if you really need to take a break and return to it later.

This is also one of those games where it's actually worth completing all the achievements outside of showing off, as much like in VS, each one also unlocks in-game content such as new cards, gems and characters. You're actually being rewarded for your efforts, which is something I really wish more games nowadays did with achievements. That said, it only took me about 32 hours to complete everything, but I'm sure this game will get post-launch support that adds more content and unlocks.

Not only is this a great expansion of the Vampire Survivors series that converts the bullet-heaven formula into a fun deck builder, it's also a good gateway game to get into the genre while also having enough depth for more experienced players to enjoy. It's simple enough for newcomers to enjoy but not so simple that veterans of the genre will quickly find boring. If you're still thinking to yourself "I'm interested in this", don't wait for a sale - it's worth every cent! My words are certainly not empty when I say this, mankind really needs more games such as this.

But enough talk, have at this game!

It has a lot of the same things as Vampire Survivors: unlockable heroes, enemies, weapons, weapon combos, etc. but the frantic pace of Vampire Survivors made it deeply engaging even though everything about it is really simple
This game, on the other hand, is a TOUCH* slower and as soon as you realize how to build for whoever you're playing as, the game becomes almost trivial. (Yes Vampire Survivors similar can become trivial but eventually you'll get overwhelmed until/unless you're in the god mode endgame.

My 3rd and consecutive runs were very easy. The only thing that ever killed me was the Devil for beating a round. The bosses barely ever hit me once, much less twice, before I won. At that point I wasnt even playing my cards myself, I was just clicking "random BS go!" and would get a W. 
Some of the characters are broken if you just want to do some quick farming (especially once you unlock a 'party')
I am hoping much like Vampire Survivors, this gets some love that makes it one of those 'must have' games but as it stands, its a fairly weak game set in the VS universe.

"Just one more run" isn't a suggestion, it's a threat.

If you thought Vampire Survivors was the ultimate productivity killer, poncle just doubled down. Vampire Crawlers takes that familiar, dopamine-soaked pixel aesthetic and shoves it into a fast-paced deck-building roguelite that feels like Slay the Spire on caffeine and holy water.

The Good
The Combo System: Unlike traditional deck-builders where you're constantly mathing out every turn, Crawlers encourages momentum. Playing cards in specific sequences (0-cost into 1-cost into 2-cost) creates explosive synergies that make you feel like a god of the dungeon.

Active Gameplay: Survivors was the king of "walking in circles," but here you’re actually pressing buttons. Choosing your path on the overworld and managing your mana adds a layer of strategy that keeps you engaged without losing that chaotic "screens-full-of-fireballs" charm.

Build Variety: Between the gems that add passive effects to your cards and the different Crawlers (characters) you can unlock, the theorycrafting is surprisingly deep for a game that costs less than a fancy sandwich.

Steam Deck Perfection: It runs like a dream. It’s the perfect "pick up for 15 minutes, accidentally play for 3 hours" game.

The Not-So-Good
100% is Quick: If you’re a completionist, you can probably max out the achievements in about 20 hours. It’s shorter than the original Survivors, but given the price and the "Early Days" feel, I expect plenty of free DLC updates.

Final Verdict
It’s addictive, it’s chaotic, and it’s dirt cheap. It doesn’t try to replace the original; it just gives you a new way to lose your sleep schedule to pixels. If you like roguelites even a little bit, this is an essential pick-up.

Better than Survivors, both in the core gameplay loop and in the fact that it knows when to end. Survivors is 100+ hours of content that gets stale about 15 hours in, while this is 30 hours of content that gets stale shortly before the 30 hour mark. It carries the same (temporary) addictiveness of its predecessor, but also the same problem of mind-numbing visuals and repetition. Just like Survivors, Crawlers is very, very, VERY easy to break, as the combo mechanic is incredibly easy to abuse. Whether its Crawler abilities that draw extra cards or Attractorb cards (I used both), its very easy to have your entire deck in front of you. Combine this with a consistent source of wild cards- I personally used Song of Mana/Mannaja and the Coin Card gem- and you'll be racking up insanely high combos that guarantee the enemy will never take a turn. While this is satisfying at first, it got old fast. Luckily, I unlocked the final achievement around the time I got tired of the gameplay, so I can at least say I had a good time the whole way through. Get it on sale, this isn't worth 10 bucks.

The graphics and overall aesthetic are also incredible, full of character and originality, and the visual and sound effects work great together to tie everything together and make it feel really satisfying. I honestly didn’t even notice the hours going by lol.

The only downside I’ve found is that it’s way too easy to end up with “god runs” where you basically have infinite turns and armor and enemies can’t do anything about it. You don’t even have to try to force it, which honestly makes some runs feel a bit repetitive and boring towards the end.

This game probably isn't quite for me. I can see how it can be fun but not for me.

In my opinin after the first 5 hours you've seen it all. Every run feels like a copy paste of the previous because the enemies don't really change their behavior at all which was fine for Vampire Survivors but for this game it doesn't quite work for me. Every run ends up feeling the exact same which I think is in part due to the combo system where you gain combo if you play cards in mana order (can be influenced in a few ways) which makes me look less at what cards do and more just how far you can push the combo because it makes each card more effective as you build more combo.

I think the game would be more interesting if there were different ways to build combos you could choose which would make you actually think more about what cards to pick for your deck beyond what mana cost they have. I think crawlers (characters) should also have a bit more variety in how they play one way or the other. At least to me it felt like some characters were OP because they have a card draw mechanic built into them and others just buff a stat up which felt less impactful early on. I know you can later on have multiple characters in the same run so probably that is where the scaling becomes better but it would still not make the gameplay feel different because ultimately the goal would still be to build the combo up and I already had a near infinite combo without trying.

I think building as high combo as possible is basically where all the fun of the game lies. If you want a very chill deckbuilder that doesn't require super much thought I think this is a great game. Just sit back, relax and turn brain off for the most part (just a little thought needed) but I think there's deck builders out there that are more interesting than this one.

Cara, esse jogo me pegou de jeito. Comecei achando que ia ser "só mais um spin-off" e já faz horas que não consigo largar.
A mecânica de combos com as cartas em ordem crescente de mana é genial — você começa a run fraquinho, vai montando a mão, e de repente tá encadeando 15 cartas numa sequência que apaga a tela inteira de inimigos. Aquela sensação de snowball que faz o Vampire Survivors ser tão viciante? Tá tudo aqui, só que com uma camada tática por cima que deixa tudo mais satisfatório ainda.
Tô jogando no Steam Deck OLED e é perfeito. O visual pixel art com aquele estilo PS1 nessa tela OLED é uma coisa linda — os dungeons escuros com os detalhes dourados ficam incríveis. E por ser por turnos, roda liso, sem travamento nenhum, ideal pra sessões no sofá ou na cama antes de dormir.
A parte de dungeon crawling também me surpreendeu muito. Explorar os andares, bater de cabeça nos baús, encontrar a pá pra ir pro próximo floor... tem uma vibe de descoberta constante que te mantém curioso o tempo todo.
Pra quem curtiu Vampire Survivors, é compra obrigatória. Mas honestamente? Mesmo quem nunca jogou o original vai se viciar. A poncle mais uma vez entregou algo especial.

I was a little bit worried, at first, when I saw in the trailer, that the game was now a deck-builder. 
But I can say with absolute certainty, that the vibes that made Vampire Survivors are not suffering from the genre change in the slightest. I am even willing to argue that the change was a stroke of genius and made the gameplay loop even more refined and focused. 
If you liked the picking-and-choosing of items and combinations in Vampire Survivors, then you will absolutely love Vampire Crawlers. 

Be aware: The game starts small, and I was afraid it might end up being shallow after the first couple of minutes, because there were only, like, 5 cards available. But the game opens up very quickly the more you play. After each dungeon, you can easily get something like 5-10 new unlocks and after just a few dungeons, the possibilities and available combinations absolutely explode. 

Eventually, you will run around with functionally unlimited mana, combos in the hundreds and game-breaking infinite(*) loops that put you into an absolute power trip!

I can rarely recommend a game as wholeheartedly as I can Vampire Crawlers.


*Make sure to not accidentally shatter your cards, or be prepared to face the consequences

А теперь к минусам, коих для меня тут вагон: 
- маленький противный момент - отсутствие кнопки выхода из игры… На самом деле не сильно критично, но очень раздражало, что при нажатии на ESC сразу залетаешь на выбор локации, будто бы это вообще не имеет никакого смысла 
- теперь самое веселое - геймплей. Я бы описал его простым словом “braindead”.  
Игра абсолютно не имеет стратегического разнообразия. Некоторые комбинации героев вообще неиграбельны, в то время как остальные могут с просто максимально ублюдским набором карт  разносить всех и вся.  
С картами ситуация аналогичная: есть просто максимально бесполезные карты, и их, я бы сказал, в разы больше, чем вообще полезных. На данный момент ВСЮ ИГРУ можно пройти через No future и билдом в количество + дамаг. 
Каждая забег одинаковый, в среднем любой забег выглядит следующим образом: 
Чистим первый этаж -> на лвл апах берем книги, броню, орбы -> из сундуков выбиваем вайлды -> имеем всех и вся, ибо у нас на первом ходу каждого файта уже можно будет собрать бесконечное комбо, а, как мы знаем, враги нас атакуют только после конца хода))) И таким образом выглядит каждый забег.  
Сюда же относим великолепную механику КОМБО, которая просто напрочь не имеет смысла. Вся суть - прожимать карты по росту маны, а если упираемся в предел, то кастуем вайлд и по новой, и так пока не набьем большое комбо, чтобы финальным штрихом прожать одну атакующую карту и всех разнести. И так в каждом файте. Самое главное, что вообще без разницы какие карты прожимать (за редким исключением конечно, за маной следить в начале комбо иногда приходится), главное, чтобы они возрастали по мане. И это просто напрочь убивает любую стратегическую механику, если поначалу я пытался придумывать, в каком порядке и что кастовать, то по мере прохождения это просто утратило весь смысл.

The presentation and aesthetic of Vampire Crawlers is incredibly charming. The pixel art, 3D imaginings of Vampire-Survivors' maps, and sound effects all work extremely effectively in conjunction to form the basis of what the game is ultimately trying to achieve.

Some people have criticized the re-use of sprites from Vampire Survivors, but this isn't an issue I necessarily have with the game. On the other hand, after my hundreds of hours in Vampire Survivors, the return of these classic enemies was yet another thing I welcomed from this spin-off. Vampire Survivors spawned a genre for a reason- that entire game is flash after flash of brilliance, and hearing Poncle announce a new in-universe title understandably excited many people. Vampire Survivors carries more than enough on its bones to be worthy of establishing an IP.

I love Poncle, and Luca Galante's work has earned much of my respect over the years for the dedication he's shown not just to Vampire Survivors, but the 2020s indie-game scene as a whole. Rare is a developer who sells a classic game for the mere price of $5. Rarer is one who actually pulls off good crossover DLCs. They're cheap as hell, too. It's a beautiful trend in an ever-greedier industry, and I applaud them not just for their actions, but how they've influenced other studios to similar approaches.

And as much good as I have to say about Poncle, I regrettably cannot recommend this game.

The deckbuilding system is flashier than it is deep. The combo system is something, but in of itself does nothing more than vaguely ask for a minimum level of engagement from the player. There were very few times where I felt like the order in which I casted my cards actually mattered. Some cards, like the Attractorb, initially bought some excitement to my deck, but pretty quickly it turned into a pattern of cast, draw more, cast. Overall, the deckbuilding lacks genuine strategy, which sucks, because that's the minimum requirement for any good card game. It's almost like the devs know this, too- why else would there be a cast-all button? Clearly the deckbuilding wasn't intended for anyone who's ever played an actual card game. 

I'd be more okay with this mindless approach to deck building if the game embraced it in the way Vampire Survivors embraced its chaotic, power-scaling, nature, with builds quickly going exponential. Vampire Crawlers does not do this, and instead needlessly limits itself and therefore, your experience of it. A good example of this is the card-shattering system.

While the maps are charming on the surface, moving around them is actually pretty boring and there's almost nothing in terms of skill expression here. With the exception of finding occasional chests and food in some areas, it felt like how I moved around the map **really** didn't ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ matter which is extra frustrating given how much I really do like the style of them. The movement of Vampire Survivors presents far more mechanical depth than that of Crawlers- And that's about as basic as you get when it comes to movement in a 2D game.

Enemy Variety is nonexistent. Yes, different sprites have different HP and damage values but it never goes beyond that. Vampire survivors gets away with it, as it's an aspect of the genre it started, but this game doesn't. I'd hope for more interesting interactions between the enemies and cards, as any deckbuilder should do, but it's not there, yet.

The game isn't awful. The game attempts to be a dungeon crawler, deckbuilder, and roguelite, all at the same time, but it does none of these things particularly well. I think most of my gripes could be addressed relatively easily with a few patches, it doesn't seem like there's anything wrong with the core of what Poncle's going for. I wish the best for its success. I do not recommend it.

I'll join the small minority of negative reviews. I was a huge fan of the OG Vampire Survivors. I love deckbuilder roguelikes passionately. The review underneath might sound very negative, but I will preface everything by saying that the bones of Vampire Crawlers are good. The game has a lot of space to improve.

The game is fine. But that is it. Definitely the price point is not high, but the balance is very busted (this can change later of course) and the game turns into a slog extremely quickly.

I dislike the grindy meta-progression, I dislike the fact that (near?) infinite decks exist so easily, and that they require insane amounts of time to play, while not being particularly mentally challenging. I made an uncountable hundred card plays during my third run, and the game was essentially solved, but for maximal meta-progression I would have to play even more cards, completely pointlessly otherwise.

"Players will optimize the fun out of a game if given a chance" I definitely lived through that very early in the game, and I usually am a fan of optimization.

Some peeves: The combos cards create are not particularly engaging, and there is just too much busy work. Animations are slow and I didn't find options to speed them up enough. I hate that I have to play through hours of easy levels to get a challenge.

I definitely think there are the makings of a good game here. I can't recommend it, because in the end it boils down to my few runs feeling either shallow in gameplay depth or just boring. Most games that really make it in the roguelite deckbuilder genre have more engaging mechanics and require engaging the mind more actively. Right now the game is at an unthankful half way point between the casino experience of the original Vampire Survivor without the real time engagement, and the fun part of all time classics like Slay the Spire 2 and Monster Train 2. The middle between these two doesn't seem to work currently, maybe in the future.

Quite possibly the best casual Roguelite Deckbuilder currently available.

Vampire Crawler is based on the previous hit indie videogame vampire survivors which was focused on destroying massive hordes of enemies with a random selection of “choose 1 of 3” items to enhance your fighting capabilities against the creatures of the night. However, even though the genre has swapped from Action Roguelike to Deckbuilder, I have rarely ever seen a more faithful and fun adaptation in the gaming industry than Vampire Crawlers.

The game manages to take almost everything from the previous entry and somehow all the items, characters, mechanics, etc. still feel and play like their original counterparts.
This is extremely fascinating to me simply because it’s a complete genre shift, and they still accomplished this feeling of familiarity while being something entirely different gameplay wise.

I would usually start describing the gameplay by now, but Vampire Crawler is already a treat before the game even really starts. Once you get past the main menu screen, you will have to complete a basic tutorial on how the game works, and then you get send to the village which serves as your “hub area” for choosing upgrades, what characters to play and other useful sections. The Village is designed in such a fun way that it makes navigating the different parts very refreshing in comparison to other titles of its genre.

Before I go over to gameplay, I also want to mention my personal highlight of this game and that is its music. The soundtrack is incredible and fits the game’s theme and aesthetic perfectly. A super fun listening experience from start to finish that genuinely surprised me.

Now, let’s talk gameplay and there is a lot to cover here.
On paper, the concept is pretty simple. You get a set of cards based on the character you picked and then defeat rows and groups of enemies to gain experience points or gold. Most cards need mana to be played and once you’re out of mana, your turn ends and the enemies then get to attack or move forward. Once the enemy conducts their action, you draw a certain amount of cards and your mana replenishes again. This process repeats itself once the foe is vanquished or your life points have been reduced to zero (with no Tirajisú left) and you will be send back to the village. Just like most other Roguelite Deckbuilders, knowing which cards to pick and when to use them is crucial to be most efficient with mana usage, and Vampire Crawler is no stranger to that concept, they take the concept even further actually by adding a mechanic called “combos”. For example by playing a card that has a mana cost of 1, every card with mana value 2 in your hand gains a slight increase in its capability (more dmg, armor, etc.). After playing the 2 mana card, all 3 mana cards now get an increase to their stats. If you’re able to rack up the combo further and further upwards, these bonus stats can reach ridiculous amounts, but only if the chain of combos hasn’t been broken by a card with a lower, equal or too high mana cost value than the previous one. The amount of run customization in this game is actual insanity, since you can add on different abilities and effects on the cards and card-evolutions are brought back as well from the previous game, which is achieved by combining mostly 2 specific cards together into a high-cost nuke everything card.

During your run, you gain exp from slaying enemies and some other sources and that exp is then used to gain more cards from a selection of 3–4 random unlocked cards. Earned Gold will be brought back to the village to spend on new characters, status upgrades or giving cards more customization slots. The goal is to complete one floor after another to progress through the many different levels and reach the final boss of the level.

Runs can get crazy really fast, but the first few hours are relatively tame compared to mid- and especially late- game. This is where I am going to make my first and only mayor complaint about this game and that has to be its completion. All the achievements are extremely easy to obtain, and I finished it in around 20 hours with all achievements already under my belt. Now, for 10 bucks, it’s still 100% worth every penny since the time you spend playing it is incredibly fun and rewarding. However, if your trying to find a roguelite/roguelike to sink your teeth in and get challenged, this probably isn’t what you’re looking for.

In conclusion, Vampire Crawler is a perfect mix of its predecessor with the deckbuilder genre, accompanied by a great soundtrack and so many unique design and gameplay choices, which makes it one of the best released indie games of this year and definitely worth checking out.

And here I thought my Vampire survivors addiction was over...

This is a card game from Poncle so you can expect insane game breaking combos. Sadly, that's a double edged sword.

Because it's really fun when you realize how OP it is when you unlock and add the +2 crawlers to your party, equip the Chain link Arcana, and get your hands on some wildcards. The early gameplay is really fun. And my God the music department is still cooking!

But the problem is the same as it was with Vampire survivors, the lack of visual clarity and some huge lack of QoL features. 
This is not an issue in the early-mid game, but when you get to the endgame around Cappella Magna (or maybe even before), you start drawing your entire deck and it's a mess.
First of all, the game doesn't track your deck. Like, at all. There's a 'Deck pile' but that has a very different function and doesn't track mana, nor does it sort cards. 

But the worst is the way the game handles the Hand.
The End gameplay loop is something like this:
Fight an enemy, level up, get a card, Tab out to the notepad and update your own deck tracker. 
Enter bossfight. While fighting the trash you have an opportunity to buff yourself, because when the boss comes, you get hit after every 6th card. So what you will be doing is this: 
- Phase 1: Drawing your entire deck. Move all combat and single use buff cards to the right side ('locked cards'). The rest you arrange by mana, these will become your 'Pool'. Select from the 'Pool' a set of cards based on mana cost. -2, -1, 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6. one of each, draw, mana and buffs mixed for a rotation. I call these the 'Active cards'.
Start playing the 'Active cards' from lowest mana cost to highest, then a wildcard, repeat. Once you played a card a couple times, it cracks. If that happens you move the card to the 'locked' section of your hand. And replace the cracked ones with same cost cards.
When you draw new cards, they go to the left side of your hand. 
So your hand looks like this while playing: 'Active cards' | 'Pool' | 'Locked Cards' Now, I named and explained these sections because I will come back to them later. 
- Phase 2: When you have enough Might, Amount, Growth, Greed, etc you will switch to combat mode.
You do change your 'Active cards' from utility to combat cards and some mana or draw if needed. 
Oneshot the boss and get Ultra Mega Overkill. Good Times.

Now my problem with this is that there's absolutely no need for all the cards to be in one place. 
Lategame becomes a surgery simulator. Because you can't see nor select your cards easily/fast enough. It's gruelingly slow and frustrating (I spent from hours to up to half a day on single runs). Stressful too because selecting cards is quite janky and you will constantly be worried that you will missclick and ruin your 100+ combo. 
The UI doesn't help either. It's squished in the middle, with wasted free space on the sides, The HP indicator is covering the left side of the hand. And the cards are a bit too big, and they are way too crammed. 
'Active cards' the 'Pool' and 'Locked Cards' should be separated, but at least the 'Locked cards' should be able to be hidden. If not separating

And it needs a proper deck tracker. The current one only shows the full deck out of combat. In combat your cards are all over the place, Deck pile, discard pile, cards above the discard pile, Neither does track the cards by amount of mana, which is baffling, since the entire gameplay loop is about playing cards in ascending mana cost. I had to spend so much time to constantly update my own deck tracker in a notepad. 

These really ruined the fun of this game for me. By the end I was just rushing through the last couple achievements, and uninstalled it with relief. I finished it in almost 60 hours when it should've been 15-20. And the only reason it took this long is because of the lack of deck tracking, the janky card selection, and the horrible visual clarity.
I can recommend this game, but it desperately needs a couple of Quality of Life changes.

このゲームを知人におすすめするかと聞かれれば、正直なところ答えは「No」です。

Steamでは「圧倒的に好評」という評価になっていますが、個人的にはやや過剰評価気味に感じました。実際、デッキビルド型ローグライクとしては発想自体は面白いものの、全体的なボリュームとビルドの幅が明らかに不足しています。

ゲームの基本システムは、マナコスト順にカードを繋げてコンボ倍率を伸ばし、ダメージや防御を強化していくものですが、やることはほぼ一貫しており、「リソースを増やしてコンボ→ステータス強化→高火力で押し切る」という流れに収束します。
特にワイルドカードの存在によってコンボの自由度が高すぎるため、ビルドの最適解が早い段階で見えてしまい、結果としてプレイの幅が狭く感じられます。

難易度も全体的にかなり低く、ステージクリア自体は10時間前後で到達可能です。序盤〜中盤はテンポよく楽しめるものの、ゲーム後半にかけては作業感が強くなりやすく「戦略が単調になりやすい」のが現状です。

ステージ3あたりまでは新鮮さもあり非常に面白いのですが、ステージ4以降はほぼ同じプレイの繰り返しになり、ゲーム体験としての伸びが感じにくくなります。

またエンドコンテンツについても、既にインフレしているビルドをさらに強化していくだけの設計になっており、長期的なモチベーションには繋がりにくい印象で、現状では繰り返し遊ぶ理由が弱いです。

総評として、アイデアや瞬間的な中毒性は確かに優れているものの、ゲームとしての厚みや長期的なリプレイ性には課題が残る作品です。序盤〜中盤を軽く楽しむ分には良いですが、デッキビルダーとして深く遊びたい人には物足りなさを感じる可能性が高いと思います。

Cependant, Vampire Crawlers ne mérite absolument pas tout l'engouement qu'il suscite.
En effet, il se contente simplement de transposer l'univers de Vampire Survivors dans un Dungeon Crawler à base de deck building. Il transpose son univers, mais aussi sa simplicité déconcertante.
Oui, il le fait, mais non, ça n'en fait pas un bon Dungeon Crawler.

Pourquoi ?
Parce qu'autant la simplicité de Vampire Survivors en faisait sa qualité première, autant pour un Dungeon Crawler avec du deck building il montre vite ses limites. C'est extrêmement répétitif, il y a peu de cartes différentes et ça m'a laissé sur ma fin en tant que theory crafter. Les stratégies sont limitées et pas très intéressantes.

Conclusion
Ce qui fonctionne dans un Survivor like ne marche dans le genre qu'il a choisi.
Si je devais le définir en quelques mots, ça serait : le Dungeon Crawler pour les nuls.
Slay the Spire est bien plus profond que Vampire Crawler.
Mon évaluation, pas aussi négative qu'il le semblerait, est plus présente pour apporter une note dissonante que pour descendre un jeu sympa, mais vraiment simpliste.

The various attributes of each character is easy to understand, as well as the cards' abilities, arcanas, and upgrades; barrier to entry for newbies is low. I just rolled credits on this game last evening, this is one of the many great games on my growing list of games I've enjoyed so far this year.  I love the card mechanics (the dungeon crawling is a lil simple but works) and how the game just feels like Vampire Survivors (presentation, unlocks and general chaos).  Pretty addictive with build theory crafting and I like how you can create card and character synergies that make you feel like a god (or even get torn down when they don't work lol).  Music is also great, as it is mostly banging remixes of the VS soundtracks.  The only negatives I have for this game has to do with the handling of your active hand when you have way too many cards.  It is hard sometimes to see the cards all at once, and even select cards with the mouse (mouse cursor sometimes gets stuck on some cards for some reason, and I have accidentally clicked the wrong one on occasion).  To alleviate this, i flipped to playing with the controller, which made selecting and viewing cards better but not fully solve it.  Other then that I love the game and highly recommend it!

"200 saatten fazla oynadığım ve dopamin reseptörlerimi uğruna feda ettiğim Vampire Survivors oyununun bir yan oyunu mu çıktı, düşünmeye bile gerek yok havada kaparım" diyerek aldığım bu oyun, ilginç şekilde beni fena kendisine bağladı. Sebepleriyle beraber gelin aşağıda birlikte irdeleyelim;

Bu oyun VS oyununa kıyasla sıra tabanlı bir kart oyunu, bambaşka bir türde aslında, ama eğer ki VS oynadıysanız ve az çok aşinaysanız oyundaki her şey size çok tanıdık gelecektir. Upgradeler, karakterler, silahlar, güçlendirmeler, hepsi direkt olarak VS oyunundan alınma, ama öyle bir noktaya getirilmiş ki oyun hem size tanıdık geliyor, hem de bambaşka bir oyun hissiyatı verebiliyor. Normalde kart oyunları bildiğiniz üzere herkese hitap etmez, çünkü tümü "sen vur, ben vurayım, en çok vuran kazansın" döngüsünden kolay kolay çıkamazlar. Çok sevilen Slay the Spire dahi bu döngüden nasibini almış, bu da mesela beni oyundan soğutmuştu. Ama bu oyunda durum bambaşka, kart oyunu döngüsüne o kadar çok ekleme yapılmış ki, şöyle söyleyeyim kendi oyun stilinizi komple kendiniz yaratmakta özgürsünüz. Nasıl mı, gelin anlatayım;

Oyun içinde kırmızı renkli saldırı kartları, sarı renkli upgrade kartları, mavi renkli defans kartları gibi renk renk sınıflandırılmış kartlar bulunmakta. Bu kartların her birinin işlevi, saldırı ve savunma şekilleri tamamen farklı, ama o farkın üzerine siz de karta eklemelerde bulunabiliyorsunuz. Her kartın üzerinde Gem adı verilen slotlar yer almakta, buralara envai çeşit Gem'lerden istediğinizi ekleyerek kartı her türlü şekle büründürmekte özgürsünüz, tabi şans da burada önemli bir faktör çünkü bu Gem'ler oyun içinde rasgele çıkıyor ve çıkanlar arasından seçim yapıyorsunuz, ama bunu manipüle etme yöntemi de var oraya da geleceğim. Gem çeşitler çok ama çok fazla, ben size birkaç tanesini fazla spoiler olmadan söylersem, kombo sayacına her şekilde katkı vermenizi sağlayan, manadan puan yememesini sağlayan, düşman öldürdüğünde para kazandıran, fazladan kart çektiren, kombo sayacını ters çeviren, mana kazandıran, can yenileten, düşman canının bir kısmını hasar olarak yanstan gibi gibi,  daha onlarcası var. bu şekilde, bunlar en basit örnekleri sadece. Bunları peki nasıl nadirliğini manipüle ediyoruz, çok güzel soru burada da devreye Blacksmith giriyor, Blacksmith sayesinde kartların hem gem slotu sayısını arttırabilirsiniz, hem de istediğiniz gem'leri daha sık, istemediklerinizin daha nadir gözükmesini sağlayabilirsiniz. Tabi bu işlemlerin hepsi para, para da oyun içinde kazanılıyor.

Gem mantığını anladık, peki mana sistemi nasıl? Her kart oyununda olduğu gibi burada da bir mana sistemi bulunmakta, kartların mana puanları oluyor, ve bunları gözeterek oynamanız gerekiyor. Burada yeni eklenen kısım, kombo sayacının aritmetik olarak manalar kullanıldığında devreye girmesi sistemi. Yani mana puanı 0, 1, 2, 3, sonra kombodan etkilenmeyen bir kart, 1, 2, gibi gibi kullanırsanız her kullanıldığında kartların faydaları kat kat artıyor. Can veren kartlar daha fazla can veriyor, hasar vuran kartlar daha fazla hasar vuruyor. Bu sistem öyle bir tatmin edici ki, bir noktadan sonra sırf "önce hangisini oynasam, hangi kartın puanının artması bana daha faydalı" taktiğini kurmak bile oyuna direkt olarak sizi bağlıyor.

Bitti mi hayır, daha Arcana'lar var. VS oynayanlar bilir Arcana sistemi oyuna bakış açınızı komple değiştirmenize olanak sağlardı. Burada da aynı işi görüyor. Oyunun başında bir arcana kartı seçiyorsunuz, sonrasında karşınızıa çıkarsa başka arcana kartlarını da alarak birbirleri ile kombolayabiliyorsunuz. Seçenek burada da oldukça fazla, can yenilemeyi 2 katına çıkaran da var, manayı elden ele aktaran, her 100 kartta mana arttıran gibi gibi çok var. Bunları da kurcalarsanız oyunlarınızda size oldukça faydalı olacaktır.

Bitti mi, hayır. Oyun içinde bolca sandık, toplanabilir, kart karşılığı güçlendirmeler, ve daha birçok şey bulabiliyorsunuz. Bunların içinde de yine evrim sistemi de yer almakta. Evrim sistemi VS ile neredeyse aynı, silahlar ve pasifler de aynı olduğundan yabancılık çekmiyorsunuz. Belirli bir silah ve pasif kart elinizde varsa ikisini birleştirip size evrimleşmiş daha güçlü bir versiyonunu sunuyor. Genellikle 2, 3, 4 mana olduğuundan oyun başında kullanmak bu kartları zor olsa da, haritalardaki 3. veya 4. katlarda artık mana sorunu azaldığından iyice tadını çıkarmaya başlıyorsunuz.

Bitti mi, yine hayır. Onlarca karakter içerisinden 3 karaktere kadar (upgrade kısmından güçlendirmesini aldıktan sonra 3 oluyor) seçerek o karakterlerin özelliklerinden faydalanabiliyorsunuz. Hem Crawler kartı denilen karakterin kendine has bir kartı bulunuyor, hem de her karakterin belirli bir kart türü ve rengine karşı verdiği bir bonus oluyor. Bunları da birbiri ile bağladığınızda yine çok tatmin edici sonuçlar alabiliyorsunuz.

Bitti mi, yine ve yine hayır. Oyunda bolca harita bulunuyor, haritaların temaları farklı olsa da bir noktadan sonra aynı şeyi yapma hissiyatı sizi buluyor, ama yine de bu oyundan sıkılmanıza izin vermiyor. VS oyununa kıyasla bu sefer haritaların zorluk seviyeleri numaralandırılmış, bu da bir haritaya girmeden önce (en azından ilk seferde) size hazırlık imkanı tanıyor. Oyun belli aşamalarda sizi fazlasıyla zorluyor, kolay bir oyun olduğunu düşünüyorsanız düşünmeyin bence. İlk 2 3 saat zaten sistemi tanıma, ve haritalarda debelenmek ile para toplayıp yeni özellikleri açmakla geçiyor. Belli bir noktadan sonra kırılma eşiği geliyor ve işte o noktada eğlence katsayısı da giderek artıyor.

Sonuç olarak, hayatımda oynadığım en iyi rogue like diyemem, hala Vampire Survivors oyunu benim için çok daha özel, ama bu oyun da özellikle ileride gelişime açık potansiyeliyle beni fazlasıyla heyecanlandırıyor. Kim bilir, bir gün belki buna da 200 saat gömmüş olurum.

Caveat: I don't care for deck-building games... I would even go as far as to say that I actively hate them in some instances. 

That being said, I love the original Vampire Survivors and I love dungeon crawlers in general, so there's enough here for me to overlook the fact that it's a card game/deck builder because of how similar it is to the addictive slot-machine nature of Survivors; borrowing the same characters, weapons, sound effects, music, etc. It also still has the "please break me" systems-heavy design that the original has and shares with games like Disgaea.

For $10, it's hard not to recommend this game. If I can get close to $1/per hour of entertainment out of any game, then I consider the game to be successful. I unconsciously put 12 and half hours into this game just this past weekend alone and I'm still actively thinking about what combinations of weapons and power-ups I want to use next, and what builds might work better than others.

Progression feels good, mechanics are slowly introduced as to not overwhelm and the pace is good. Meta progression feels good as well. There are a LOT of very good strategies, and it's satisfying to discover new ways to break the game. OST is good, I like the different variations of the village theme depending which building you're in, nice touch!

Also hits that nostalgia vibe for us 80's and 90's gamers as it's very reminiscent of oldschool dungeon crawlers like Ultima. My only complaint is I wish it would have taken me longer to beat the game, but that's a good problem to have!

All things said, highly recommend if you like deckbuilders with roguelite and roguelike elements. At this price it's a no-brainer.

I've already played this enough (3-4 runs on the first map) to be able to give a postive recommendation. This is a fantastically crafted representation of Vampire Survivors in the deck builder genre. Only now its addictive properties are combined with systems that provide the player with a lot more agency and the satisfaction of tactical planning/execution. 

If deck builders appeal to you you'll definitely enjoy this. However, I'm not yet certain it ranks with other genre superstars (Slay the Spire series, Balatro) as many others are suggesting. There's no doubt a ton to do here (as VS before it, it's phenomenal value), and the first-person dungeon crawler perspective is a wonderful rendition of the Vampire Survivors world, but I'm not sure that it doesn't adhere too strictly to its conventions. E.g. you can't target enemies (though this provides for its own type of planning), the wave-based format of the fights doesn't seem necessary, and a lot of the incremental upgrades don't feel impactful. 

As well, the interface needs some refining in terms of delivering information to the player. E.g. how do I examine a character's traits outside of the inn? How can I decode what the active status yellow card effects are in battle after I've played them? They have icons and quantities, but no mouse-over text. It would be nice to have that aid until I memorize them. Lastly, the exit button's placement should be more obvious.

Highly Recommend – 8/10

After spending 14 hours in the demo and another 24.5 hours in the full game, I can confidently give this an 8/10.

The game successfully captures what made Vampire Survivors great, but presents it in a completely different way. If you were turned off by Vampire Survivors' fast-paced gameplay but still liked the overall concept, this is a perfect alternative. The turn-based system lets you carefully plan each move, making every decision feel meaningful.

Like most roguelikes/roguelites, the early game can be tough, but it becomes more manageable as you progress. The game really opens up once you can run more than one crawler, this is where things get fun. The synergies can get absolutely wild, and you can create some of the most broken builds imaginable.

I'll admit the Milkmaid is probably overtuned and may get nerfed (at least at the time of review), but she's not required. All crawlers have strong builds, with or without her.

Game Mechanics

The weapon system from Vampire Survivors has been adapted into a card-based format. Just like in Vampire Survivors, weapons can be combined into stronger forms, and the combinations are the same here.

What this game adds is a slot system. Weapons can have slots where you insert gems that provide boosts, bonuses, and other perks. Later in the game, you can increase the number of slots, allowing some cards to hold two or even three gems, which adds another layer of strategy.

Pros:

Lets you play at your own pace; you can pause or quit mid-run and return anytime
Familiar weapon system with enough new mechanics to feel fresh
Turn-based gameplay makes each decision feel important and strategic

Cons:

Music isn't as polished as Vampire Survivors (though it may grow on me)
Some crawlers, weapons, and arcanes feel significantly stronger than others, balance could use some work
Hard caps on stats (e.g., +1000% EXP bonus) feel limiting and unnecessary

好玩，爽游。
这游戏给卡牌的强度给的很慷慨，像抽牌、回费、加费、打连击、无限、回复、血上限等等方面。这种情况下拿大卡组依然会有鬼抽的情况。靠血量、回复、加血上限等手段是能很轻松的接下这些伤害的，只有想打死神时才需要增强稳定性和专门构筑。
游戏对达成无限出牌的玩家的针对手段也让人眼前一亮。同一张牌在同一回合打超过五次就会破碎掉，并召唤特殊boss紫色死神，每碎一张就多召唤一个。破碎掉的卡牌相当于就在整局游戏中删掉了。大小boss的行动点设计也很有趣，玩家在回合中没打出几张牌，boss就会强制行动一次，但不会中断玩家的连击和回合，相当于每用几张牌就会插入一次boss的行动。
玩了几十小时，感觉超大卡组的稳定性确实要差。
一局游戏为了达成牌组40张牌的成绩，抓了42张牌，每层打小怪都会有两三次第一回合鬼抽的情况，还出现过第二回合也鬼抽的情况，掉了些血。有回复手段倒也不怕，血量能被打好几个回合，卡组一启动就能回满血量。
隔壁的塔2也玩了几十小时，但beta版未经考虑就直接上了正版后就没怎么玩了。与其被逼着玩大卡组，每回合抽五张牌、打五张数值没法和怪物比的牌，在艰难的蠕动过程中被连续鬼抽害死或者被boss打死，不如玩这个爽。而且塔2后续的更新也确实体现出塔2制作人对玩家的恶意和江郎才尽
如何在限制无限流的同时，不破坏玩家爽感，相比隔壁某某2代，这个游戏的制作组给出了教科书般的的答案

全成就通关后回来评价一下，是个很不错的游戏。

虽然是肉鸽卡牌，但本质上还是RPG线性推进的模式，有成长有结局，所以才比较合适给到全程爽感的体验。对比隔壁杀戮尖塔那种希望你一直反复玩几百小时的真正肉鸽的运营模式就不一样，所以对比这游戏，有些拉踩杀戮尖塔的评论并不是很合适。

这游戏前期靠12345W12345循环，中期就是多角色连携加续航和输出，打到大后期有能力杀红死神了后，数值就彻底崩坏了，最后就是无限W通关（前提是升满宝石铺），但这游戏有最终结局，所以大后期崩坏也是一种爽，但也注定了这游戏就是一次性通关游戏，重玩价值并不高。这种设定其实还是很讨巧的，一般玩家也不会愿意动辄几百小时反复玩一个重复规则的单机（特指杀戮尖塔1&2----至少1代我打到10难就没劲继续玩了），所以控制在30小时以内通关的一次性游戏也非常棒，至少整体的游玩体验一直都很棒，也不会一直无聊。

总结：作为一个一次性游玩的卡牌游戏是非常棒的选择，全成就了也就可以封档删游了，但全程的游戏体验不会让你感觉亏本，是真可以全程爽完结局的。

PS：唯一的诟病就是金币控制实在太抠门，不打到最后一个角色（W增加金币收益）是完全赚不到大笔钱的，而宝石铺和铁匠铺如果要控制到最完美的状态（只保留五张卡其他全封印：红进化+冰冻+万能+抽卡+10%削弱），计算下来要花费1000万以上！这一点实在坐牢，建议开发者后续能优化一下就最好！

I've tried Slay the Spire etc, and deckbuilders haven't always sat quite right with me - they're fun and all, but I don't get that 'just one more run' bug like I feel I'm supposed to. That's not the case with Crawlers; the style, music and characters from Survivors are all there, but to me the gameplay loop is even more polished. It started off slowly, but then at some point my go-to strategy clicked into place and I feverishly completed run after run ticking off all the achievements like a crazy person.

As of writing this, I have 73.8 hours in Vampire Survivors, with just some DLC challenges left to do, but the 26 hours I spent on this game in the past 3 days have been exponentially (and surprisingly!) more fun somehow?!

This is easily my favourite game of the past several years, and I desperately hope that it receives many, many updates. Even though I've finished what it currently has to offer, I'm going to keep it on my steam deck forever alongside Survivors <3
For VS enjoyers, this will not disappoint at only $10.

In this drastically oversaturated genre, I really don't see why you'd want to play this over even just Slay the Spire 1. I'd like to think I'm just not the target audience and it's meant for a much more casual player, but, truthfully, I just can't see anyone finding this that much more entertaining than any of the other dozens and dozens of games just like this that have increasing difficulties allowing you to ease your way into thinknig hard, or just staying on low difficulties and doing the card game equivalent of button mashing.

That's ultimately my impression of the game - incentivized button mashing.

[h1] 9/10 [/h1]
[h3] 通关并且全成就，毋庸置疑，吸血鬼爬行者是我今年玩过最爽快的游戏！ [/h3]

[h2] 先说优点： [/h2]
1、上手简单，五分钟就能学会这游戏核心玩法，按顺序打牌叠连击。
2、正反馈拉满，这游戏特别牛的一点就是玩家的每一份付出都有回报，你刚觉得这一关打的很艰难，马上就会解锁强力道具，又进入爽游循环。
3、各种牌的打击感，特效做的很好，配合音效，完全能体会到割草的爽感。
4、局外成长顶中顶，只需要一种金币资源即可。对比同样重局外养成的喵喵，喵喵的局外养成是又麻烦又肝度爆炸。
5、最喜欢的一点就是打完这游戏最难的BOSS红死神后会解锁隐藏角色，离谱的数值但完全的合理，打过最强的BOSS玩家就会变成最强。红死神令人安心的数值，完全给玩家的各种玩法兜底。

[h2] 再说缺点： [/h2]
1、转向操作多余，刚开始玩很不适应。
2、居然没有卡牌排序功能，尤其是手里卡一多很容易打错断连击，后续能加个遗物给手里卡排序吗？
3、虽然有倍速遗物，但是来的太晚了，这游戏还是很需要倍速的，毕竟重复度比较高。

[h2] 总结： [/h2]
[h3] 简单直接的把爽感带给玩家，游玩过程中很难忍住不笑。通关吸血鬼爬行者的感觉就像，肚子很饿，正好旁边有家餐馆，进去美美饱餐一顿 ，走出店门一阵凉风吹过然后长舒一口气，心里想着明天是否还要再来这里，步伐已经踏出去准备迎接下午的休闲时光了。[/h3]

最初はいろんなカードの組み合わせを見つけたり、手札やマナなどリソース管理が楽しいですが以下戦法を見つけた途端１戦が長い作業ゲーになってしまい最短でボスを目指すだけになってしまいます。
[spoiler] プニャーラをメインキャラ、ジオヴァーナをサブキャラにして封魔の書や魔導書（マナ回復）を採用するだけで爆発的に回転率が上がり、パッシブで運やリロールを積んでおけばワイルドカードを大量に積めて100コンボなどが簡単にできます。コンボが伸びればバフ・ダメージ・防御全てが強力になるので同じカードを4~5回使うと出てくる死神も簡単に倒せてしまいます [/spoiler]
相手のターンにならず、ただ数字の順番通り出すだけのゲームとなってしまいました。

我其实一直觉得吸血鬼幸存者和土豆兄弟除了皮相类似外很难算是同种类型的游戏，因为只要上手品两把就能完全明白土豆本体几乎是肉鸽数值打磨巅峰的宝珠，而幸存者则更像是一块粗糙朴实的——[b]冰[/b]。幸存者的成瘾性在于，它在数值上非常取巧地给了玩家相当大的冗余甚至是超模的局外成长，让玩家只要运用最简单的抉择策略就能享受到超出预期的战力快感，到最后甚至要引入各种死神来半强制地让玩家从满屏眼花缭乱的伤害数字跳动中抽离；所以与其说幸存者开创了刷怪波走位生存的玩法[strike]（其实这套是从magic survival学的）[/strike]，不如说它发扬了叠技能合超武然后战力大跃升的爽游泛式。[b][strike]（诶，其实这套也是从magic survival学的）[/strike][/b]成功复刻这种泛式所能提供的爽度只要看看去年的球比伦战纪就可见一斑，甚至鼠鼠登月这种甜品级类幸存者都能轻松收获千评好评如潮。

于是同样的我也不觉得爬行者是典型的肉鸽卡牌，因为它也只使用了最最基础的策略抉择，拒绝了投入风险管理以及一切激烈的小学生四则运算；整体节奏依然是选定合适道具-凑升级合超武-战力大爆炸的老虎机循环，从相当前期开始就能品尝到数值碾毙怪群的爽感，而后期甚至连凑超武都是多余了，单纯靠数值和强力宝石就能把死神提起来打。在很多时候甚至会觉得这游戏简直用编排好的脚本就能自己把自己玩下去（就像球比伦的沉思者那样），考虑到牌多还得挑半天沉思者可能玩的更快更好（）无论是前期解锁双枪姐配魔力歌谣姐直接开转，还是狂敲钱袋宝石万能宝石让手牌撑爆到看不清但也没关系因为只要一直点下去连击就不会断，再或者电表倒转爆刷鞭尸奖励镶嵌金钱攻击宝石接着一百万匹马力吸血鬼爆破拳！！！游戏对玩家的多巴胺操控出神入化，虽然只是一直选更强的武器宝石然后游戏自己就打赢了，但这种欣快感竟然诡异地和某些正统肉鸽运筹经营一整局的体验不遑多让。说实话电子游戏把斯金纳箱发掘到这种程度我已经很难想象传统老虎机柏青哥除了能爆真米外还能在成瘾性和爽感上有什么优势了（虽然不知道这是好事还是坏事）。不得不赞叹luca作为一个前菠菜行业编写老虎机的程序员，竟然能把这份玩弄斯金纳箱的经验如此丝滑地融入到甜蜜无害的游戏中——[b]两次[/b]，一次还能说是妙手偶得但两次则无疑是货真价实的天才了。

不过万幸的是这种爽感会随着游戏时间的积累越来越昏沉平淡，直到发现从某个时刻开始打的所有局几乎都是同一种体验，从而形成一种meta的刹车。（同样很巧的是这时候差不多也玩完了可以无缝转进下一个游戏了好耶
Includes all the dairy and dairy accessories. 10/10, would churn again.

Very fun deck builder, though very simplistic. Honestly the better parts of the game are when you're first starting and learning the systems with limited resources, but once you get enough unlocks the game snaps like a twig, and you end up struggling more sifting through your 30+ card hand than you actually do with any of the encounters, which given this a card game Vampire Survivors, that's the point, but the other issue comes with the lack of variety and content. 

Unlike Survivors there's less variety in weapons, and you're moreso picking cards for their mana values for amassing absurd combos. I also think that including a mass amount of Wing cards on the last stage severely kneecaps the difficulty and makes the last level and final boss feel more like a victory lap than a test of your mastery with the systems, but that's more a nitpick than a criticism.

You can do much worse for $10, and the game is still really enjoyable and well put together. Just don't go in expecting a wealth of depth or content like Survivors or other roguelike deck builders.

Боссы не ощущаются в лейтгейме, потому что ты уже настакал себе миллиард всего и проиграть становиться тяжелее, чем победить. В итоге каждый босс файт выглядит так: добрал стартовый разгон, закрыл джокером, под комбо натянул армор, добрался в 15 карт, снова надел армор, а потом просто на всю котлету закинул в лицо бедного вампирславыча. То есть придумать что-то реально интересное может и можно, да толку в этом никакого нет. Целью постоянно будет анлимитед стак комбо с последующим выстрелом в лицо сотню раз. Позже вообще появится усиление, наносящее урон, равный твоей голде, в рандом таргет (в слеучае с боссом - только в него) и игра перестает быть интересной.

1.手感顺滑，和一代一样，一只手爽玩。

2.和初代的创新有类似的溯源，古老框架引入肉鸽meta进程。

3.和初代解决的最核心的问题相似，打磨一套机制（数字阶梯），这套机制足够简单-低门槛高天花板，且充分融合其他已有机制。并且这里对低门槛的要求极高，一只手、简单数字、简单移动等等，他的最内核机制规则一句话可以讲清楚；这是坚实地基，天才的设计+勤奋迭代的结果，是这位作者最牛逼的技艺。

4.成熟的内容编排，类似于一代，数值、内容展开的方式从容。内部结构合理。

5.Slots的体验，用卡牌表达，装在古早视听环境中。一个可参与的策略类的开大奖。

[b]Vampire Crawlers[/b] is a great little vacation from deckbuilders that all walk the tight line of balance-obsession, opting to just go hog-wild. You’re not going to so much play a well-tuned game as much as you are going to enjoy breaking the hell out of it. I was skeptical with the demo being limited to what we could do with it, but after blazing through, 100%’ing all current content, I can safely say that this was a special treat, despite the lower difficulty and expectation from the player.

The core loop of Crawlers that keeps it engaging is learning to manipulate your combos to maximize your output. The early game will [i]feel[/i] like you aren’t getting a lot out of your synergies. A card draw here, some increased damage there. You’ll start off thinking that the bracer is the best card in the game, only to never use it again by the mid and late game as your card draw explodes out of control. Your 3 energy will go from “is this all I have?” to “I have 999 energy and can’t even end my turn because I keep cycling my 40 card deck forever.” Combo 4? How about combo 100. 5,000 gold to slot a card seem daunting? Try returning home with 2 million, one-shotting even Death by the end-game, and now you can just buy every upgrade and roflstomp as a stress relief after work.

To me, the game is a lot more engaging than Vampire Survivors, since you reach a point in VS where you just shut your brain completely off and let the game solve itself. I chased achievements in that game when it first came out, only to burn out on it that even the likes of Castlevania DLC couldn’t keep me glued, since the loop pretty much exhausted itself by the time I finished the base game content.

With Crawlers, I’m actually left itching for more, and am sad that I’ve now run out of content. It’ll likely be amusing to hop on and shatter it some more, but I want to savor the game like a fine wine, so I’ll gladly return to it when it gets its inevitable DLC with new cards to draft. I highly recommend the game if you like deckbuilding but want a more chilled, laid-back experience when you aren’t number-crunching in the more intense ones out there.

This game is so much fun.  It's weird to say this but I think playing the cards should be fun and too many games turn the playing of the cards into a chore where the fun is supposed to be winning the run.   This nails getting fun cards that are doing multiple things and building combo's.  The gem system is brilliant in that it takes a fairly good but non-priority card like bracer and suddenly it could be increasing luck each time I play it so even if my hand size is plenty big I still want to keep playing it.  Being able to add gem slots and take along multiple crawlers is great fun.  Wildcard cycles and using wildcard gems to turn less played cards into combo builders make the turn by turn gameplay exciting.  In later floors of an area I can be so engrossed in sequencing all the cards to apply a massive amount of life or bump up xp gain that I accidentally play an attack that kills everything in front of me, even when it wasn't what I was going for it's still glorious.

The Town has a couple of places to visit when there, and you will find more as you progress, changing accompanying heros, called Creepers, and selecting different bonuses will make each run different and fun.

All in all, a fantastic addition to the Vampie Crawlers world.

При том, что в сам Vampire Survivors не играл, а только в клоны, все задавался вопросом: "а кто тут, собственно, вампир, кроме летучих мышей, и где?", но было приятно убивать нечисть.) Чуть более по уму начал складывать колоду только под конец игры, но до этого можно было спокойно играть только правильно считая стоимость карт. Баланс карт хороший и комбо собирать удобно, но на 100% от рандома, что тебя завалит, это не спасёт. В общем, и для казуала (если требование к знанию математики ещё относится к "казуальщине") и для плюс-минус стратега тут место более чем найдётся, а сессия длится, в среднем - около часа плюс-минус, а значит можно за короткий перерыв пройти "подземелье" и с чистой (в прямом и переносном смысле) душой отлипнуть от монитора.)

Day 1 thoughts:

Game leaves a strong impression, the gameplay loop is really fun and solid, poncle hit the mark again with the same magic that Vampire Survivors has

I love the 2D 3D perspective and moving around, the setting and atmosphere of the home town, it's something I wish Vampire Survivors had. The voice acting adds a TON of character and charm to existing characters. I didn't really care that much about some of them before, but I like them all now more than just from pure design/aesthetic, which was really the only thing I liked for most of the characters in VS.

Excited to see if the same kind of wild, cryptic secrets VS has are in this one once I clear more stages and unlock more characters and goodies.

Issues so far, which are subjective and something I need more perspective and experience to see if I feel the same about after, are that enemy health bloat starts feeling absurd from a beginner's perspective starting at difficulty 6, which might just mean I need to learn how to get better single-target dps in my decks for bosses (plus it's a roguelike where I'm meant to start weak and become much stronger through permanent upgrades between runs, so I'm waiting to form more of a verdict on this), and runs can take really long if I play more thoroughly (1h 30m+). Which is fine, it's just hard to set aside time for a run, compared to VS where I know my run limit will be between 15m to 30m, which makes it a convenient lunch break game. That's just the nature of a turn-based dungeon crawler game paired with my tendency to play games thoroughly, and I'll have to see how I feel about it long-term.

Excellent pickup for under 10 dollars, I can't wait to be off work after this review to play more.

Vampire Survivors was one of my all time favorite games, I know that doesn't mean much when your looking for if a game is worth buying, but I wasn't sure about this one. I don't like card based or turned based games. However much like survivors this game is much more than just a card battler. It can be fast or slow fully up to how you want to play it, if you are familiar with survivors there are lots of tid-bits in this game that make you smile. Gameplay is simply play your cards, manage your mana, and try to kill the enemies in front of you. Then navigate the dungeon floor and choose to avoid or battle the others monsters around. It can be played with a controller or one handed making it cozy or serious. You can focus as much as you want or idly click and play while watching something on the tv. All in all my review is biased because I am a fan of the developers, but this game made me like card battlers and i am excited to see what other games come from this one. 10/10 would eat the floor chicken..

I loved Vampire Survivors and I really wanted to like Vampire Crawlers. The art is cool (largely reused from Vampire Survivors, but if it ain't broke...) and the music sound/design is pretty good. The actual gameplay and deck building aspects are extremely lacking, however. It is too easy to just faceroll through the dungeons once you find a deck combo that works (which is really easy to do). With the right characters, you don't need to really even find any cards at all - the starting deck is good enough, which is a huge issue. Similarly, the attack/defend cards and powerups don't feel nearly unique enough. The deckbuilding mechanics simply aren't there.

6/10

The game is, in many respects, quite good. Compared to the demo there is ample content, and much of it fun to interact with. However, the over-tuned anti-"infinite" mechanics seem to discourage things that the achievements and Crawler playstyles directly encourage, making for a rather mixed messaging approach to building decks. Took me just shy of 30 hours to get all of the achievements for the game, after about 3 days of play. To say the game is addicting would be quite the understatement. 

Despite that, I do appreciate how respectful of your time it is from the ease of unlocking new characters, stages, as well as how the double-time/hurry mechanic is implemented and how early you unlock it. Not unlike the original, in that regard. While the artstyle and whole vibe and mechanics of the game are undoubtedly ripped straight from the original, they did put a fair bit of thought into making that original game format shine in this Slay the Spire-esque spin-off and it shows. The effects are gorgeous as ever, the controls are gratifyingly tight and the music wonderfully remixed and remastered for this new entry.

I've had a blast with the game for the time I spent with it and can wholeheartedly recommend it to anyone wanting to scratch that 'watching the number go up' type itch these guys do so well. If you liked the original, then you'll enjoy your time with this one, as well.

Vampire Crawlers is just as addictive as Vampire Survivors. Pretty much everything I love about Survivors is still here, except the gameplay is completely different. This time it’s a deck‑building dungeon crawler instead of a bullet‑hell game. Both styles feel very different but equally satisfying.

The progression is similar, and I find it satisfying because it’s not too difficult but not too easy. There are a lot of unlockables—so much that there’s always something new to chase, whether it’s characters, levels, arcana cards, relics, or weapon evolutions. I’ve personally been enjoying going for the achievements since there are over 150 challenges, and working through them is a great way to discover everything the game has to offer.

For me, it’s one of those games where an hour feels like ten minutes. This morning, I sat down planning to play for 20 minutes… and two hours went by lol. Overall, great game and I can't wait to play more!

Original review:
This is a difficult game to rate tbh.
At first it's a fantastic game, a fresh retake on the Vampire Survivor game that somehow manage to port the real time dopamine hit of the original game into a turn based card game.

The gameplay itself is pretty sleek and smart and I expect it to produce a couple of copy cats like Vampire Survivor did...

...and then you play a bit more of the game and realize that game is hironically enough way flatter than it's 2D counter part.

Unlike Survivor, Crawler is way more easily broken in ways that the game can't deal.
The game is extremely linear and offers little replayability, basically acting like a giant tutorial for an infite mode that doesn't exist, and once you reach the end of the game, you realize the only viable strategy is to push infinite combos where you use wildcards to reset combochain infinitely.

It wouldn't be that bad if unlike Suvivor, the gameplay of Crawler was zeny and allowed you to go with the flow, but instead the game becomes grindy and repetitive.

Once you finish the game then you are done. There isn't really any point in trying other builds because levels are fixed and repetitive and (as far as I know) the only extra challenge at the end is to kill the reaper which seem to be just a pretty annoying check on how many CC you've brough with you.

It's a bit sad really since Survivor itself was the perfect "infinite replayability and build diversity" game, while this one isn't at all.

I'll still give it a thumbs up because of how entertaining the game is for at least it's first half, and for it's very low price, but just know before you buy that a Survivor style game this isn't and expect to be completely done with it after 15h.

There are a variety of zones (there is a world map) very different from each other with multiple tiers.

Mixed feelings for Vampire Crawlers. It's really quite impressive how much of the Vampire Survivors experience has been adapted into a card game, but at the same time it's really disappointing just how much it is pretty much just like Vampire Survivors. The colorful visuals, banging soundtrack and fun of chasing unlocks just like in VS is pretty much perfectly replicated, but as a card game the depth is severely lacking. After unlocking everything, I feel pretty much no itch to keep playing because there is very little strategy and variety in the deckbuilding aspects of the game. 

This is such a stark contrast to something like Slay the Spire 2 where the gameplay itself keeps you coming back long after you've unlocked everything. I think if you've never played any deckbuilding roguelike before, and you've never played Vampire Survivors, this is a good first intro to both of those things. For me, the experience quickly felt hollow and repetitive. Like Vampire Survivors, I'll look forward to revisiting after a bunch of new things get added so I can chase more unlocks, but until then, I find it hard to recommend.

I have no idea how, but they somehow managed to make yet another extremely addicting game.

I have so many things to say about this game, but I will try to keep this short.

First of all, if you're looking for a Slay the Spire level of depth, this game isn't it. It's very casual. Yet.
Second, this game is very well designed. You obviously start kind of weak, so you start mixing and tinkering your build until you find the sweet spot you've been looking for, only to find it's flawed in the next zone. This goes on and on until you realize you need more money to make it better to progress further. You also realize you didn't completely clear the early zones. So you go back and just wreck havoc, which is super satisfying because you spent the time building your optimal build. If you play MMOs you know what I'm talking about, because everybody tries to actively remove this aspect of their games.

There were some missing QOL features I wanted to mention, but I eventually figured out it's actually either a deliberate choice by the devs or an artifact you can find which unlocks the respective features. Which, again, goes back to how well designed this games is. I mean the things I didn't like turned out to be fixable by playing the game!

I guess my only gripe with the game is it does eventually become a bit easy once you figured out an optimal build as you collect new artifacts and power ups. But then again, you will hit this point after about 10, 15 or 20 hours of gameplay depending on how quick you are. And it's 10 dollars. Soooooo... Yeah. Again, very reasonable. There is also a way to circumvent certain restrictions, but I assume that's not intentional and will be fixed in the future.

This game has so much potential for future features. Maybe an enemy that hunts you through the map? An endless mode? Restricted build/rock-paper-scissors mode? Random encounters? I could even imagine a coop mode where you both have to progress through the map to unlock the next level. Some of these might as well be in the game but I just haven't found them yet, lol.

It's crack 2.  Even more addictive than the first game.

✅ Pontos Fortes
-Gameplay extremamente viciante, com progressão rápida e recompensadora a cada run.
Sistema de upgrades e evoluções que cria combinações absurdas e divertidas.
-Sensação constante de poder crescente, saindo do caos para o controle total da tela.
-Loop simples de aprender, mas difícil de largar perfeito pra sessões longas sem perceber o tempo passar.
-Variedade de builds que muda completamente a forma de jogar a cada tentativa.
-Estilo visual minimalista que funciona muito bem na clareza da ação.
-Trilha sonora e ritmo que aumentam a tensão conforme os inimigos se acumulam.
-Conteúdo desbloqueável em grande quantidade, incentivando progressão contínua.
-Mecânicas que recompensam experimentação e risco.
-Ideal tanto pra jogar casualmente quanto pra “só mais uma run” que vira horas.

⚠️ Pontos Fracos
-Pode se tornar repetitivo após muitas runs seguidas.
-Falta de narrativa mais profunda para quem busca história envolvente.
-Visual simples pode não agradar quem prefere gráficos mais elaborados.
-Em momentos avançados, a tela pode ficar poluída demais.
-Algumas builds acabam sendo muito mais fortes que outras, reduzindo variedade real.
-Progressão pode parecer lenta para quem não gosta de grind.
-Curva de aprendizado indireta (muitas coisas o jogo não explica claramente).

🎯 Pra quem é
-Fãs de jogos roguelike e progressão infinita.
-Quem gosta de gameplay viciante e recompensador.
-Jogadores que curtem testar builds e combinações diferentes.
-Quem prefere ação intensa e direta, sem muita enrolação.
-Pessoas que gostam de jogos simples, mas com profundidade mecânica.
-Quem quer algo perfeito pra jogar “só um pouco” e acabar ficando horas.

🧾 Nota: 9/10 Isso aqui não é só um jogo, é praticamente uma armadilha disfarçada de diversão. Você entra achando que vai jogar “rapidinho” e, quando percebe, já tá completamente preso tentando mais uma run perfeita. Extremamente simples por fora, mas absurdamente difícil de largar, quase um crime de tão viciante 🧛‍♂️🔥

I really loved my time with vampire crawlers but I have a few major issues with it.

They mostly boil down to the enemies are very uninteractive and the ways to stop them are very limited. As such, a lot of builds end up feeling similar even if they are very different. I was very surprised there was no crit gem that lets non-crit weapons crit. I think evolutions also make the game worse because while huge damage upgrades, it feels like some are just different weapons. Scythe is the biggest problem here since Axe is very useful with knockback but Scythe just doesn't have that. Survivability is an issue as your combo is either so high you get 100+ armor instantly or you dont get enough to survive boss hits. 

I think an arcana that lets you summon gems as enemies would also really open up boss fights. because since boss are just 1 on 1, a lot of options like O'Sole's weapon and on kill effects become useless.

Overall I think the game needs a lot of work but is a very strong baseline! 

I am naming all of these problems because literally everything else about the game is so well put together its hard to accurately describe how satisfying, fast, and wild this game can be.

I mean it is good, and it has great game feel / responsiveness, but I don't feel like it has much skill expression. Maybe I haven't played for long enough (or maybe I'm just amazing at the game :P) but it feels like each hand you draw has one pretty obvious 'solution' for how to play it. This is especially true with the mana-stacking combo mechanic, which again feels great for player empowerment, but it makes each hand feel even more rigid and solved: obviously I'm going to play the cards I drew in order from lowest to highest pretty much all of the time.

I don't feel like I'm ever making very many meaningful choices in a dungeon crawl, unlike games like Slay the Spire where you are constantly weighing choices against each other. Sometimes you have to consider what gems to add to which cards, but even then it feels pretty simple.

And this imprecision is apparent in how the game is presented, too. Things are not very clear and deterministic for the player.
* It's not explained how any attack card chooses its targets (especially its splash damage) because for example what's the difference between how the fire wand chooses damage targets versus how the cross does so?
* You don't know how much health enemies have, so decisions like deciding to armor-up or to build up combo to go for the kill are just total guesses.
* You don't know how attacks like peachone or santa water are going to deal their extra damage in upcoming turns, so you can't really plan around it beyond guesstimating.

So I guess I'd recommend it for a power-trip kinda game, but not really as an actual strategic deckbuilder? I'll keep playing it and don't regret the purchase, but I would definitely go for other deckbuilders instead if I want an actual brain-on experience.

Having now completed the game 100% here are my thoughts:
Incredibly fun and addictive like Survivors was in early/mid game. Interesting card design, fun puzzle solving to try to maximise your combo and with that damage/armor output. OST and sound design in general is great. Playing high damage cards feels impactful with tons of projectiles being sent out. Boss design is also made interesting by allowing them to attack after you play a certain number of cards instead of just after you end your turn (and other things but no spoilers 😇).

BUT as soon as you get to later game the stages become way too reliant on you having some sort of an "infinite" scaling build or abusing certain crawlers to consistently one shot encounters. If you don't, you just die. These combos are incredibly easy to get by just grabbing some hand size, card draw and wildcards so progressing shouldn't be an issue. However, since getting these combos is so easy, they don't feel satisfying when you do manage to pull them off. 

These combos have another downside: clutter. You will be drawing your entire deck constantly making your hand incredibly cluttered and finding a specific card you want to play quite time consuming. Combining this with the fact that you'll need to play a LOT of cards each encounter to pull these combos off, each encounter drags out much longer than they need to.

If there was a neutral rating on steam, I would use that. Early and midgame are great but don't bother with late game unless you want to 100% this. Took me maybe 8 to 10 hours to get to the point where I had to start using "infinites" so consider if the price point is right for that playtime for you. Just hoping this receives balance changes for late game.

Absolut fantastisch, so schnell hab ich wahrscheinlich länger kein Grind-game mehr 100% durchgezogen - Die Mischung aus Deckbuilding roguelike und dem Dopamin-kick von super langen Combos und spaßigen Synergies knallt hier wirklich richtig rein.

Die Progressionskurve ist wirklich enorm smooth, quasi nach jedem Run schaltet man irgendwelche Upgrades, Powerups, neue Chars oder Deckideen frei und kurz vorm Ende des Completionist-runs fängt man an ENORM viel Gold pro Run zu bekommen um einfach den restlichen Shop aufzukaufen. Das Pacing bezüglich der Upgrades ist wirklich perfekt gebalanced, stark!


In vielen anderen Games könnte man vielleicht kritisieren, dass es etwas zu easy wird sobald man einmal den Dreh raus (und mehr als einen Crawler aktiv) hat, aber hier ist der Fokus so offensichtlich nur auf dem spaßigen Bauen von insanen Decks, dass das als Negativpunkt nicht wirklich zieht und vielleicht mit zukünftigen Updates und Endgame-content geändert wird. 

Das was es machen will, macht es fast perfekt, einzig alleine das Rumlaufen und Abarbeiten von normalen Gegnern kann relativ schnell etwas zäh und träge werden, weil das sonstige Gametempo super hoch ist. Wäre das Game wesentlich schwerer, würde das definitv mehr stören, so ist es eher selten dass man als halbwegs erfahrerer Deckbuilding-Spieler irgendeinen Unlock mehr als 1-3x versuchen muss. 

Auch etwas schade ist es, dass sich irgendwann quasi jedes Deck ziemlich ähnlich spielt: unendlich lange Combos und Mana-generation + Scaling, wodurch die einzige Gefahr die automatischen Konterangriffe und/oder das Zerstören der Karten durch die Anti-infinite Mechanik wird. Cool ist hier aber, dass der "Tod" durch zu lange Combos überraschend leicht zu töten ist und eher früh im Run eine Gefahr ist.

Ich warte freudig auf mehr Content, aber wäre selbst ohne Updates sehr zufrieden mit der Erfahrung.

Poncle cooked hard here and then just gave up in the second half. 

I had to update my review to be negative because while it is an amazing dopamine filled experience at the start, it becomes incredibly easy, if not tedious at the end. Vampire Survivors did have that a bit but at least there are runs you need to do for unlocks where you are a bit weaker. The endgame is just filled with destroying everything turn 1 with quasi-infinite cycles without thinking much.

The game seems to be bugged at the moment or something because some cards never show up for me so I am unable to complete my collection in the upgrades section. There is no info in-game hinting at how you get them to appear but players say you apparently need to play their more basic versions for them to appear first. However, after multiple runs of cycling hundreds of the lower tiered versions to try to get something like Rainbow Armor, they have yet to appear and I have lost so much time for nothing. I saw some other players complain about not finding things so I suspect their is either a bug or there is some probability they need to fix.

Fights are just not really complex enough and you'll learn over time how much you might have been overthinking things. Red Death for example is insanely easy to beat with any freeze card in play if you understand how freezing and unfreezing works. And then you learn you shouldn't have even bothered with doing that because you can consistently one shot him once you are far enough in progression.

I would recommend playing the demo. All the good parts of the game lie there and it's free. I hope Poncle actually fixes issues with the endgame.

局内体验：爽不到之余也缺乏策略 
[b] 既不能让人真正放空大脑，也没有提供足够高的策略密度。[/b]

构筑可控性低、牌效低，启动慢：
这三个缺点导致游戏前期充斥大量的重复劳动，能明显感觉到游戏在用数值控制成长速度。局内缺乏稳定回血机会，一旦中途发现构筑方向有问题，能调整的选项也比较单调。

数值对抗多，策略变化少：
玩家侧：策略单调且重复性高，不存在核心额外机制，成长拖沓。怪物侧：行为单调，缺乏特色，大多纯数值。缺乏策略深度。

UX设计：糟糕 
1.怪物意图：没有用足够突出的颜色表示，导致我前几局完全没看懂敌人到底要干什么。

2.按钮缺乏按键提示：明明这是一款可以用左手完成大部分操作的游戏，这点本来值得表扬，但游戏几乎不教玩家快捷键，新玩家上手时只能被迫左右开弓，自己摸索。

3.卡牌管理：缺乏有效的筛选和排序功能，想确认牌库构筑时非常难受。对于一款构筑游戏来说，这种基础信息整理做不好，体验会被持续磨损。

These Devs already know how to trigger crazy amounts of Dopamine with Survivors. Crawlers perfected it. Though there's a bunch of stuff missing from Survivors' base game that I hope to see come here. But its still a fun as hell video game that can stand on its two legs.

It's a fun and wild romp where you get to build up a busted deck to snap the universe in half with nearly endless combos and try to circumvent the threats that appear when you try to go EVEN FURTHER BEYOND!!!

Honestly I'm not sure what to say about it, the trailer shows exactly what the game is. It's fun, it's got a bit of dungeon crawling shenanigans, but some parts can feel like a show-stopper where you need to stop and go buy some upgrades to get a more viable deck or better scaling stats. And even once you've got end-game stats, the last few levels can take more than an hour to successfully complete and you as far as I can tell, there's no way to save progress mid-level to return to it later.

Overall, I had fun. If it gets extensions or DLCs I might try those. But I don't think I would want to play the game again from a blank file.
